intro:
There is not enough information available about the plant Chenopodiastrum helenense. However, I can provide you with general information about the Chenopodiaceae family, which Chenopodiastrum helenense belongs to. The Chenopodiaceae family consists of leafy annual or perennial plants, commonly known as goosefoots or oraches, which are characterized by their flat or rounded leaves and small flowers. 

For Chenopodium album (not Chenopodiastrum helenense), the most common member of the Chenopodiaceae family, the health benefits include its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties, which may help in treating various conditions such as arthritis, digestive issues, and skin problems.

The bioactive constituents of Chenopodium album include alkaloids (such as chenopodium and aspidospermine), flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins. 

The most common herbal preparations made with Chenopodium album include teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions.
